thesis: World's largest publicly traded copper producer positioned at the epicenter of the global energy transition. Operations spanning Indonesia (Grasberg), North America, and South America. 2024 EBITDA of $10B (+14% YoY) with strong operational execution. Grasberg underground ramp-up progressing with world-class copper/gold grades. Critical leverage to copper structural supply deficit through 2030 driven by EVs, renewables, and AI data centers.

drivers: Grasberg achieves 1.6B lbs peak production by 2026-2027
Supply deficits widen faster than expected

risks: Copper falls to $3.75/lb on global recession
Indonesia imposes punitive terms or export restrictions

base_case.assumptions: Copper averages $4.50/lb (current levels); Grasberg ramps as planned through 2027; Indonesia negotiations result in status quo; Steady copper demand growth (3-4% CAGR).

catalysts.event: Indonesia Terms Negotiation — Critical regulatory milestone
Copper Price Movements — LME inventory levels

key_risks: Indonesia political/regulatory risk — government majority stakeholder through Inalum
Commodity price volatility — 50% revenue sensitivity to copper prices
Grasberg operational execution — underground ramp-up complexity
China demand slowdown — 40% of global copper demand
ESG/tailings management risks in mining operations
